qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via outputOptions.dir allows arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Adjacent sink but different root cause: this report relies on attacker-controlled outputOptions.dir, while the advisory is unsanitized outputFile.fileName traversal reaching writeOutputFile.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via incomplete sanitizeFileName allows file write outside output directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same Rollup root cause: sanitizeFileName leaves dots and slashes intact, embedded ../ survives validation, and writeOutputFile resolves outputFile.fileName outside the output directory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code injection via --plugin argument using new Function()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and sink: CLI --plugin new Function execution is distinct from Rollup output filename traversal and arbitrary file write.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Symlink resolution allows arbitrary file disclosure in bundle output
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and impact: this report targets symlink realpath resolution and file disclosure, not unsafe output filename sanitization causing writes outside the output directory.

selected_finding.title: Path traversal via incomplete sanitizeFileName allows file write outside output directory

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory-described sanitizer bypass and writeOutputFile path-resolution sink. The copied PoC ran Rollup 4.58.0 and showed a crafted emitted asset filename escaping the configured dist directory, proving the advisory arbitrary-file-write consequence.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mw96-cpmx-2vgc describes sanitizeFileName omitting dots and slashes, allowing ../ sequences in output filenames to reach writeOutputFile path.resolve and write outside the output directory.

path-traversal-sanitize-file-name/vulnerability_report.json lists src/utils/sanitizeFileName.ts, src/utils/relativeId.ts, src/utils/FileEmitter.ts, and src/rollup/rollup.ts:317 with the same embedded ../ source-to-sink path.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_2f67491a/.judge/poc_runs/path-traversal-sanitize-file-name/run.log shows Rollup version 4.58.0, PROOF FILE FOUND outside output directory, CONFIRMED not in dist, and EVOUNT_SUCCESS proof=EVOUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF_7a3f9b2c.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied Docker PoC ran Rollup 4.58.0 with an emitted asset fileName of subdir/../../EVOUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF_7a3f9b2c.txt; the verifier found the proof file outside /tmp/poc-workspace/dist and confirmed it was not present inside the output directory.
